title: Frequency
prompt: Hillbrow apartment at night, bathed in green frequency displays. Lerato hunches over radio gear, headphones clamped on. Johannesburg sprawls through slatted blinds; Nelson Mandela Bridge glows crimson. Dust motes swim in monitor light. Cables hang like thick vines from the ceiling.
narration: The headphones reek of sweat and cracked foam. One notch. Static fills the room, fine and abrasive. Another night cataloguing the empty spaces on the Southern African band. Logbook open: dates, times, weather, absolute silence. For eleven months. A bus horn wails outside. Then—a shift. A tone threading through the noise. He narrows the bandpass, fingers freezing. A voice, impossibly clear, states his name. A date. Tomorrow. A bridge will burn. A precise location to stand. Six seconds of signal, pure and direct. Then gone, swallowed by ordinary static. He removes the headphones. His hands lie flat on the desk, steady as stone. He does not write.
